entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, the crop species under study for male-fertility restoration.

entities.name: MALE-FERTILITY RESTORATION
entities.description: The genetic ability to restore pollen fertility in cytoplasmic male-sterile lines.

entities.name: A1 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific cytoplasmic-nuclear male-sterility system in sorghum commonly used for hybrid production.

entities.name: A2 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific cytoplasmic-nuclear male-sterility system in sorghum studied for genetic diversification.

entities.name: A3 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific cytoplasmic-nuclear male-sterility system in sorghum studied for genetic diversification.

entities.name: A4(M) CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific cytoplasmic-nuclear male-sterility system in sorghum studied for genetic diversification.

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSIFICATION
entities.description: The process of increasing genetic variety to produce more heterotic hybrids.

entities.name: HETEROTIC HYBRIDS
entities.description: Hybrids that exhibit superior performance compared to their parents.

entities.name: ISONUCLEAR BACKGROUNDS
entities.description: Genetic backgrounds that are identical in their nuclear DNA composition.

entities.name: F2 POPULATIONS
entities.description: The second filial generation used to observe the segregation of fertile and sterile plants.

entities.name: BC1 POPULATIONS
entities.description: The first backcross generation used to observe the segregation of fertile and sterile plants.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The environmental condition during the 2007 growing season affecting fertility expression.

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The environmental condition during the 2007 growing season affecting fertility expression.

entities.name: CHI-SQUARE TEST
entities.description: The statistical test (c2) used to determine the goodness of fit for genetic segregation ratios.

entities.name: MONOGENIC RATIOS
entities.description: Segregation ratios indicating that a trait is governed by a single gene.

entities.name: DIGENIC RATIOS
entities.description: Segregation ratios indicating that a trait is governed by two genes.

entities.name: TRIGENIC RATIOS
entities.description: Segregation ratios indicating that a trait is governed by three genes.
